title: practical english usage - Part 212
content:
You're the woman (that) I always see in my dreams.

'Casablanca' is a film (that) I watch again and again.

Instead of what or the person, we  can use less general expressions.

The person who kept a motorbike in the garden shed was Emily.

To  emphasise words that refer to people, we  use structures like the person who.

The secretary is what Jake is, actually, (   Actually, what Jake is This structure can be used to emphasise a complement.

What Emily kept in the garden shed was a motorbike.

What I need is a rest.

The  words to be emphasised are often put last: this gives them even more force.

A motorbike is what Emily kept in the garden shed.

A rest is what I need.

whɑt-dause (or similar structure) by is!was.

In this kind of cleft sentence, the words to be emphasised are connected to a 1 What      I need      is  ...

274 cleft    sentences:      What      I need      is  a  rest.

example, I'm the person / the one who's responsible.

To  avoid being either too formal or too informal in this case, we  could say, for It's you that's in the wrong, (informal) - It is you who are in the wrong, (formal) It's me that's/who's responsible, (informal) - It is I who am responsible, (formal) When  an emphasised subject is a pronoun, there are two possibilities. Compare: 2  It  is  I who     ... ; It  is   me    that    ...

Hardings secretaryds: Nor can a complement be emphasised: we  cannot say It's Jenny that Mr that-mysecfefary-thedrUl.

The  verb cannot be emphasised with this structure: we  cannot say It was sent not It was his parents who paid the fees, (     It were his parents...) not It is the students that are angry, (     .. .-that-is angry?) remains singular).

When  a plural subject is emphasised, the following verb is plural (but it is/was It was my secretary who sent...

Who is possible instead of that when a personal subject is emphasised.

It wasn't my husband that sent the bill...

Negative structures are also possible.

It was yesterday that my secretary sent the bill to Mr Harding.

(not to somebody else) It was Mr Harding that my secretary sent the bill to yesterday.

(not something else) It was the bill that my secretary sent to Mr Harding yesterday.

grammar •  274  cleft sentences:  What I need is a rest.

My first journey abroad is something I shall never forget.

The first thing was to make some coffee.

The only thing I remember is a terrible pain in my head.

All (that) I did was (to) touch the window, and it broke.

All I want is a home somewhere. All you need is love.

All (that), and expressions with thing, can be used in cleft sentences.

5  other     structures What happened was (that) the car broke down.

The car broke down.

what and the verb happen. Compare: A  whole sentence can be given extra emphasis by using a cleft structure with 4 emphasising         a  whole     sentence:      What      happened           was     that    ...

What they did, they took his money and then just vanished.

What I'll do, I'll have a word with Dan.

In a very informal style, is!was may be dropped.

What I'll do is, I'll phone Jack and ask his advice.

What she does is, she writes science fiction.

Instead of an infinitive, we  often use subject + verb in an informal style.

What he did was (to) scream. What she does is (to) write science fiction.

without to are possible.

have to use a more complicated structure with what.. . do. Infinitives with and When  we want to emphasise a verb (or an expression beginning with a verb), we 3 emphasising         verbs:    What      he   did    was    ...

Why I'm here is to talk about my plans. (More formal: The reason why I'm Spain's where we're going this year.

in the middle of a sentence.

The place, the day or the reason can be dropped in an informal style, especially The reason why Jake went to London was to see Tom.

To see Tom was the reason why Jake went to London.

- Jake went to London TO SEE TOM.

The day when Jake went to London was Tuesday.

Tuesday was the day when/that Jake went to London.

- Jake went to London ON  TUESDAY.

The place where Jake spent Tuesday was London.
